title: Kon'me
content:

The '''Kon'me''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]]. Living on the small islands that littered the surface of Bal'demnic, the Kon'me divided themselves into two prominent social classes, an upper-class that resided in luxurious bubble-like structures on oceanside cliffs, and a lower-class that lived in simple, thatch, [[Beach/Legends|beachside]] dwellings. Known to be extremely distrustful of off-worlders, the Kon'me tried to keep from involving themselves in larger [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs unless absolutely necessary, but they were unable to avoid being forced into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] when it was discovered that Bal'demnic's cliffs contained the rare [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]].

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Battle of Bal'demnic|invaded]] Bal'demnic, seeking to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the cortosis [[Ore/Legends|ore]], with the higher-class Kon'me reluctantly allowing the Separatists' occupation. However, the beachfront Kon'me were less compliant, and rebelled against the Confederate [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|battle droid army]]. During the resulting battle, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] sent a detachment of its [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] led by a number of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] to assist the Kon'me insurrection. The Kon'me rebels, however, seeing all the off-worlders as tyrannical invaders, fought back against both Confederate and Republic [[Army/Legends|armies]], eventually forcing the two intruding factions to leave Bal'demnic.

==Biology and appearance==
The Kon'me were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic]], in the [[Bak'rofsen system]] of the [[Auril sector/Legends|Auril sector]].<ref name="db" /> [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] in appearance, the Kon'me were [[Color/Legends|pink]] hued with dark spots on their bodies, and had a large neck that extended from their torsos to a pointed head. The Kon'me possessed hands with three digits each along with feet with two digits.<ref name="cw" />

==Society and culture==
The Kon'me lived in the small, tropical [[Beach/Legends|beach]] islands that dotted Bal'demnic's [[Water/Legends|watery]] surface. The species arranged themselves into two main social classes, with an upper-class that lived in glistening, bubble-like structures built on the islands' cliffs, and a lower-class that dwelt in simpler thatch, beachside dwellings.<ref name="db" />

The Kon'me were heavily distrustful of off-worlders and colonists, taking an isolationist stance and avoiding involvement in the affairs and conflicts of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="DP">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> Bal'demnic would likely have become a popular tourist destination—being considered very beautiful and attractive by many off-worlders—were it not for the Kon'me's isolationist policies, and as a result the Kon'me were viewed as an annoying and noisome species by the rest of the galaxy.<ref name="db" />

During battle, the Kon'me wore [[Armor/Legends|armor]] that included a breastplate, shoulder plates, and a helmet with a horizontal slit for their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] to see through. In conjunction with this armor, the Kon'me wore brown leggings and wrist guards.<ref name="cw" />

==History==
{{Quote|I was Ak'ghal Usar. Last lord of Urkupp, the lost dominion of Cron, and subjugator of the Kon'me. All that remains is my vengeance.|Ak'ghal Usar|<ref name="KOTFE">{{TORcite|exp=kotfe|type=general|Reclaimed Treasure|Yavin 4}}</ref>}}
Due to their separation from the rest of the galaxy, the Kon'me remained mostly undisturbed by galactic wars and events.<ref name="db" /><ref name="DP" /> Nonetheless, the species was conquered by [[Ak'ghal Usar]], a [[Dashade/Legends|Dashade]] [[Shadow Killer]] who ruled the nearby planet of [[Urkupp]], sometime before the [[Cron Supernova/Legends|Cron Supernova]] wiped out Urkupp<ref name="KOTFE" /> in [[3996 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]—the final year of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>—Confederate [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] found trace amounts of the rare [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] sheltered in Bal'demnic's island cliffs. Wanting to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the cortosis—a metal so dense it could even block [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] attacks—for their war efforts against the Republic,<ref name="db" /> and coinciding with their goal of directing Republic forces away from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="atlas" /> the Confederacy approached the upper-class Kon'me with an ultimatum: either allow the mining operations on Bal'demnic to begin or be destroyed by the Confederacy's [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|battle droid army]].<ref name="db" />

[[File:WTS-CloneWarsPlanet.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Kon'me rebels fighting against Confederate spider droids.]]
Seeing little choice in the matter, the Kon'me leaders reluctantly permitted Commerce Guild [[Miner/Legends|miners]] to start their cortosis operations on Bal'demnic. However, the lower-class Kon'me, viewing the off-world miners as invaders, [[Battle of Bal'demnic|rebelled]] against the Separatist occupation, using powerful weapons [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] to them by war profiteers hoping to take advantage of the situation.<ref name="db" /> The Kon'me insurrection set up military [[Barracks/Legends|barracks]] in the upper-class' bubble homes on the island cliffs, mounting their new high-tech [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to drive back the Separatists. In response to the rebellion, the Confederacy dispatched [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droids]] to overrun the Kon'me's positions,<ref name="cw" /> pushing Bal'demnic into chaos.<ref name="db" />

Meanwhile, Separatist transmissions regarding the situation on Bal'demnic were intercepted by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the Republic. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], not wanting the Confederacy to obtain cortosis that could be used against [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the war, dispatched a detachment of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]—led by a number of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] including [[Sha Koon]] and [[Halagad Ventor]]—to sabotage the Confederate invasion in any way possible. However, once the Republic task force landed on Bal'demnic, the Kon'me freedom fighters, seeing the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone soldiers]] and their Jedi leaders as just another [[Army/Legends|army]] of off-world invaders, opened fire on the Republic army as well as the Separatists.<ref name="db" />

Baldemnic's island terrain made it difficult for the Republic and Separatist armies to maneuver, forcing both off-world factions to retreat from the planet's surface. The Confederacy abandoned Bal'demnic altogether, while the Republic set up a small [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] defense [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] around Bal'demnic to assure that the Separatists would not try to set up another cortosis mining operation.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|This villain was called the Mekon, and I have hence named Bal'demnic's natives the Kon'me.|Arthur Papadam discusses the origin of the Kon'me name|<ref name="Arthur">{{Blog|arfmaul|7|The Making of Bal'demnic: The Island Planet}}</ref>}}
The Kon'me first appeared in the opening montage of [[Chapter 22 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 22]] of the animated television series, ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', produced by the [[Cartoon Network]]. The chapter first aired on [[March 22]], [[2005]], as part of the show's [[Clone Wars: Season Three|third season]]. The name of and a brief backstory on the Kon'me were later established by [[Arthur Papadam]], writing under the pseudonym "Arf Maul," for Bal'demnic's entry in the [[Databank (original)|Databank]], as part of the ''[[What's The Story?]]'' feature on [[StarWars.com]]. The Kon'me were also included in Bal'demnic's entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published on [[December 9]], [[2008]].

Arthur Papadam named the Kon'me species after "[[Wikipedia:The Mekon|Mekon]]," the final boss in the [[Wikipedia:Commodore 64|Commodore 64]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], [[Wikipedia:Dan Dare: Pilot of the Future|''Dan Dare: Pilot of the Future'']], released in [[1986]]. The physical appearance of the Kon'me reminded Papadam of Mekon, whom he describes as "a big green [[Alien/Legends|alien]] with a big head." This resemblance was furthered by the weapons emplacements of the Kon'me, which reminded Papadam of Mekon's own weapons platform.<ref name="Arthur" />

As a way of justifying the Kon'me's success against the Confederacy, Papadam stated that he intended the Kon'me to "boast a history of defeating several local aggressors,"<ref name="Arthur" /> although no reference to this made it into the final version of Bal'demnic's Databank entry.<ref name="db" /> The name of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was derived from the word "condemn," which Papadam hoped might give "the impression that the planet and its inhabitants are not as innocent as they might appear."<ref name="Arthur" />
